tax: Base score: Summary 10; placement 12; appearance 14; ecology or persistence 14; behavior 10; traits 12; abilities 10; background relevance 8; structural hygiene 10. Award points only for entry-specific claims. A missing section earns 0 for that section. A sentence that could be pasted unchanged onto five unrelated entries earns no detail credit.
ap: Quantitative thresholds: a full section normally has at least 45 meaningful words and at least three concrete, entry-specific details. Appearance needs at least three observable markers, such as silhouette, limb count, posture, skull, mouthparts, integument, eyeshine, scent, gait, spoor, size, or fossilizable structures. Ecology needs at least three persistence facts, such as diet, habitat, reproduction, spores, lifecycle, formation, transmission, prey, predator, symbiont, zone, or lair dependence.
beh: Penalty rules: subtract 25 for a duplicate sibling or duplicate ID; 20 for a role, caste, profession, or culture treated as a biological species; 15 for rank or parentage mismatch; 12 for a placeholder wrapper with one child and no independent definition; 10 for two or more sections made of boilerplate; 8 for missing persistence; 6 for no concrete identification markers; 5 for contradiction with a parent or sibling; 3 for unclear common/scientific naming.
traitsText: Stub detection is automatic when any two are true: total prose under 180 meaningful words; four or more scored sections below 20 percent of available points; fewer than eight concrete details across the whole entry; no ecology or persistence mechanism; no observable physical marker; or the entry mainly restates its name and parent. Automatic stubs cannot score above D+ until expanded.
abilities: High-quality detection requires all of the following before assigning A- or higher: at least 420 meaningful words; all eight content functions present; at least 24 concrete details; at least one contrast with a neighboring branch; at least one field-identification marker; a defined persistence mechanism; bounded abilities rather than vague powers; and no duplicate, role-split, or unresolved hierarchy penalty. S-range entries should exceed 650 meaningful words and 40 concrete details.
bg: Review workflow: score the base 100 points, apply penalties, cap automatic stubs at D+, cap entries with unresolved duplicate or role-split at C-, cap entries with rank mismatch at B- unless the mismatch is an accepted inferred orphan species, then assign the grade from the cutoff table. Record the weakest three categories first so revision work is measurable.
g: This guide defines a 100-point scoring system for entry quality. Score only the written entry and its immediate structural placement, not creature importance, danger, rarity, or preference. Begin every entry at 0, add points for measurable evidence, subtract penalties, then convert the final score to a letter grade.
note: Quantitative rubric only; internal scale calibration is not written into the catalogue.
